skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Create professional internal communications with reusable templates for status reports, newsletters, announcements, team updates, policy changes, and incident updates.
skill.summary: Draft clear internal updates, announcements, newsletters, and team communication plans.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution patterns, but review found static documentation templates rather than executable behavior. Backticks are Markdown formatting, HTML links are placeholders, and no prompt injection, scripts, network calls, shell execution, or secret access were found. The remaining concern is normal privacy handling for internal company content.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography indicators occur in Markdown filenames, fenced templates, placeholders, headings, and communication examples. I found no executable code path, shell command, network request, or cryptographic implementation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visible as static Markdown or HTML template text. There is no interpreter, script tag, command invocation, or external endpoint tied to these locations.

low_findings.title: Internal Information Handling Requires Care
low_findings.description: The skill is designed for status reports, incident communications, newsletters, and employee updates. Users may paste confidential project, employee, incident, or metrics data into an AI tool, so organizations should apply their normal data handling rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The purpose and templates clearly involve sensitive internal updates, incidents, blockers, metrics, and personnel recognition. This is a usage privacy concern, not evidence of malicious behavior by the skill.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often become inconsistent, too long, or hard to act on. This skill provides structured templates and guidance for clear company, team, and project communication.
actual_capabilities: Draft weekly, monthly, and project status reports with metrics, blockers, priorities, and resource links.
Create company newsletter content with leadership notes, team spotlights, recognition, events, and announcements.
Structure all-hands announcements with context, timelines, action items, FAQ sections, and follow-up plans.
Prepare team updates that cover wins, learnings, morale, upcoming work, risks, and recognition.
Choose communication channels and cadences using best practices for email, chat, wiki pages, meetings, and video.
Plan incident, policy, change management, recognition, OKR, and cross-team communications from included templates.
limitations: It does not send messages or integrate with Slack, email, wiki, or project management tools.
It does not verify company facts, policy details, dates, metrics, or legal requirements.
It cannot replace HR, legal, security, or executive approval for sensitive announcements.
Users must remove confidential or personal data when their AI tool policy requires it.

use_cases.title: Publish Executive Status Updates
use_cases.description: Turn project notes into concise status reports with progress, risks, blockers, decisions, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare Company Announcements
use_cases.description: Create all-hands, policy, or change announcements with clear context, effective dates, employee impact, and FAQ content.
use_cases.target_user: People operations and internal communications teams

use_cases.title: Build Team Newsletters
use_cases.description: Assemble recurring newsletters that highlight milestones, team spotlights, training, events, recognition, and reminders.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Weekly Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a weekly team update from these notes. Include a short summary, wins, blockers, priorities, help needed, and next steps. Keep the tone clear and professional.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite an Announcement
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this internal announcement for employees. Put the main point first, explain why it matters, list action items, and add a brief FAQ.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Change Communication
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinate a process or policy rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a communication plan for this change. Include audience segments, message sequence, channels, timing, owner roles, manager talking points, and feedback collection.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Incident Update Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an incident communication sequence for this situation. Provide initial notice, recurring update template, resolution note, post-incident summary, and stakeholder-specific guidance.

output_examples.input: Raw project notes with completed work, two blockers, a delayed milestone, and asks for leadership.
output_examples.output: A structured status report with an executive summary, progress metrics, accomplishments, risks, help needed, upcoming priorities, and owner-based action items.

output_examples.input: A draft policy change announcement that lacks context and employee instructions.
output_examples.output: A clearer announcement with the change, effective date, reason, affected groups, required actions, timeline, FAQ, and contact path.

output_examples.input: Newsletter notes from several departments, including wins, events, training, and recognition.
output_examples.output: A polished newsletter outline with section headers, short blurbs, calls to action, and placeholders for links and images.

best_practices: Start with the audience, decision, and action needed before drafting supporting details.
Use consistent sections, owners, dates, and metrics so readers can scan updates quickly.
Review sensitive communications with the right approvers before publishing them.
anti_patterns: Do not bury critical decisions or action items in long background sections.
Do not publish confidential employee, incident, financial, or customer details without approval.
Do not reuse one tone or channel for every audience and urgency level.

faq.question: What types of messages can this skill help create?
faq.answer: It supports status reports, newsletters, announcements, team updates, policy changes, incident updates, recognition posts, and cross-team updates.

faq.question: Does it send messages for me?
faq.answer: No. It only helps draft, structure, and improve communication content. You must publish through your own approved tools.

faq.question: Can it handle sensitive internal information?
faq.answer: It can structure sensitive topics, but users must follow company rules for confidential, personal, legal, security, and financial data.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for status reports, newsletters, announcements, team updates, policy updates, post-mortems, OKRs, and recognition.

faq.question: Can it adapt tone for different audiences?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance covers formal, semi-formal, and informal messaging for executives, managers, teams, and employees.

faq.question: Is this suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documentation-based and can guide drafting in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
